reasoning: Two critical fixes are identified and straightforward: (1) Change uint8_t distance to uint16_t in CompactHashTable and CompactBoolHashTable to prevent infinite loops (semantic bug); (2) Ensure delta-encoded date columns are properly decoded before filtering (correctness). Iteration 1 crashed due to the uint8_t bug, but the underlying optimization strategy (morsel-driven parallelism + compact hash tables + delta decoding) is sound. With both critical fixes applied, expect to achieve 6-8x speedup from parallelism on 64 cores, targeting ~50-100ms execution (vs 386ms baseline and 77-112ms DuckDB benchmark).
focus_areas: Fix uint8_t → uint16_t distance field in CompactHashTable (line 44) and CompactBoolHashTable (line 137) to prevent DIB overflow infinite loops

Re-enable morsel-driven parallelism with thread-local aggregation from iteration 1 (6-8x speedup potential on 64 cores)

Target execution time <100ms to approach DuckDB performance (77-112ms) and close 3.5-5x gap from baseline
strategy_notes: This is the final iteration (2/3). Once crashes are resolved, morsel-driven parallelism should deliver 6-8x speedup on 64-core system. The iteration 1 optimization strategy was correct; only the hash table implementation was buggy. Aggressive approach: apply all fixes + re-enable parallelism + compact hash tables in a single coherent implementation. No need for incremental changes — the Query Optimizer can handle all simultaneously.
notes: Critical fixes take priority. The uint16_t fix is low-risk (<2 min fix). Delta decoding verification is already implemented in iter 1; confirm it executes correctly. Then focus on execution correctness and parallelism efficiency. If execution time exceeds 200ms after fixes, investigate load imbalance across threads or inefficient aggregation merging. Do NOT attempt additional optimizations (zone maps, SIMD filtering) in iteration 2 if fixes + parallelism are not stable — stay focused on correctness and core parallelism gains.
